fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/src/validators/order.validator.ts b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..fc75283
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
+/**
+ * Order validation — pure validation functions for order operations.
+ *
+ * All functions are pure: no side effects, no database calls.
+ * Services perform lookups and pass the results to these validators.
+ */
+
+import type { Order, OrderItem, OrderStatus } from '../models/order.js';
+
+/**
+ * Allowed status transitions for orders.
+ * Each key maps to the set of statuses that an order can transition to.
+ */
+const STATUS_TRANSITIONS: Record<OrderStatus, OrderStatus[]> = {
+  pending: ['confirmed', 'cancelled'],
+  confirmed: ['shipped', 'cancelled'],
+  shipped: ['delivered'],
+  delivered: [],
+  cancelled: [],
+};
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an order exists.
+ * Throws if the order is undefined.
+ *
+ * @param order - The order record from a repository lookup.
+ * @param orderId - The ID that was looked up (used in the error message).
+ * @throws Error if the order does not exist.
+ */
+export function validateOrderExists(
+  order: Order | undefined,
+  orderId: string,
+): asserts order is Order {
+  if (!order) {
+    throw new Error(`Order not found: ${orderId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an order has at least one item.
+ *
+ * @param items - The list of order items to validate.
+ * @throws Error if the items array is empty.
+ */
+export function validateOrderHasItems(items: OrderItem[]): void {
+  if (items.length === 0) {
+    throw new Error('Order must have at least one item');
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Check whether a status transition is allowed.
+ * Returns a boolean — useful when the caller needs to handle the result
+ * without an exception.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current order status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @returns true if the transition is valid.
+ */
+export function isValidStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): boolean {
+  return STATUS_TRANSITIONS[from].includes(to);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that a status transition is allowed.
+ * Throws if the transition is not valid.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current order status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @throws Error if the transition is invalid.
+ */
+export function validateStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): void {
+  if (!isValidStatusTransition(from, to)) {
+    throw new Error(`Invalid status transition: ${from} -> ${to}`);
+  }
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bc40d8c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+/**
+ * Preferences validation — pure validation functions for user preferences operations.
+ *
+ * All functions are pure: no side effects, no database calls.
+ * Services perform lookups and pass the results to these validators.
+ */
+
+import type { UserPreferences } from '../models/user-preferences.js';
+
+/**
+ * Validate that preferences do not already exist for a user.
+ * Used when creating new preferences to prevent duplicates.
+ *
+ * @param existing - The result of looking up preferences by user ID.
+ * @param userId - The user ID being validated.
+ * @throws Error if preferences already exist for this user.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesNotExist(
+  existing: UserPreferences | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): void {
+  if (existing) {
+    throw new Error(`Preferences already exist for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that preferences exist for a user.
+ * Used when updating preferences to ensure the record is present.
+ *
+ * @param existing - The result of looking up preferences by user ID.
+ * @param userId - The user ID being validated.
+ * @throws Error if no preferences exist for this user.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesExist(
+  existing: UserPreferences | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts existing is UserPreferences {
+  if (!existing) {
+    throw new Error(`Preferences not found for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/user.validator.ts b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..fe2abe4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,62 @@
+/**
+ * User validation — pure validation functions for user operations.
+ *
+ * All functions are pure: no side effects, no database calls.
+ * Services perform lookups and pass the results to these validators.
+ *
+ * Reusable by any service that needs to validate user-related constraints
+ * (e.g., UserService, OrderService).
+ */
+
+import type { User } from '../models/user.js';
+
+/**
+ * Validate that a user exists.
+ * Throws if the user is undefined.
+ *
+ * @param user - The user record from a repository lookup.
+ * @param userId - The ID that was looked up (used in the error message).
+ * @throws Error if the user does not exist.
+ */
+export function validateUserExists(
+  user: User | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts user is User {
+  if (!user) {
+    throw new Error(`User not found: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email address is not already taken by an existing user.
+ * Used when creating a new user to enforce email uniqueness.
+ *
+ * @param existingUser - The result of looking up a user by the given email.
+ * @param email - The email address being validated.
+ * @throws Error if a user with this email already exists.
+ */
+export function validateEmailUniqueness(
+  existingUser: User | undefined,
+  email: string,
+): void {
+  if (existingUser) {
+    throw new Error(`User with email ${email} already exists`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email address is not already in use by another user.
+ * Used when updating a user's email to enforce uniqueness.
+ *
+ * @param existingUser - The result of looking up a user by the new email.
+ * @param email - The new email address being validated.
+ * @throws Error if the email is already in use.
+ */
+export function validateEmailNotInUse(
+  existingUser: User | undefined,
+  email: string,
+): void {
+  if (existingUser) {
+    throw new Error(`Email ${email} is already in use`);
+  }
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 5 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-7PHTNP.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Extract validation logic from service classes into dedicated validator modules.

Specifically:
1. Review the services in src/services/ to identify inline validation logic.
2. Create a src/validators/ directory with validator modules for each service that has inline validation.
3. Validators should be pure functions — no side effects, no database calls.
4. Update services to call validators instead of doing inline validation.
5. Add tests for the new validators.
6. Make sure all existing tests still pass after the refactoring.

Important:
- This is a refactoring — existing behavior must not change.
- Validators should be reusable across services.
- Follow existing code style and patterns.
